title: .expect() chains in frame.rs obscure failure location
description:
lichen-link/src/frame.rs:267-315 uses a state machine with .expect() calls:

```rust
transition_frame_state(&mut state, FrameProcessingState::Failed)
    .expect("empty input can fail before length read");
```

While the expect messages are descriptive, this pattern:
1. Mixes error handling (returning Err) with state tracking (the state machine)
2. Uses expect for transitions that should be infallible by construction

Consider either:
- Removing the state machine (it adds overhead but the parsing is straightforward)
- Using debug_assert! for state transition invariants instead of expect

The state machine pattern seems like over-engineering for a linear parser.
close_reason: Added file:line references to .expect() messages in frame.rs (lines 273-321)
